observed_state.summary: Settings footer remains dirty after a successful save

inferred_cause.hypothesis: post-save config rehydration re-dirties the routing-role select because equality check does not compare rehydrated value with just-saved value
uncertainty: whether other settings controls share the same re-dirty pattern

desired_state.goal: After successful save, draft equals saved config and the dirty flag clears
success_criteria: draft state equals saved state after rehydration
manual user edits still mark the footer dirty

candidate.from_state: settings footer remains dirty after save
candidate.to_state: draft equals saved config after successful save; footer dirty flag cleared

route.reason: Confirm where the controlled select onChange fires after rehydration

route.action: Add equality guard: skip onChange when incoming rehydrated value === current draft value
route.reason: Prevent re-dirty when rehydrated value is identical to the just-saved one

route.action: Add regression test that covers save + rehydrate cycle
route.reason: Lock the equality guard against future regression

steps: git revert the equality guard commit
drop the regression test if it becomes flaky
rerun unit tests to confirm pre-change behaviour

expected_evidence_after_apply.claim: draft equals saved state after save and rehydration
footer dirty flag becomes false on the dirty-footer regression scenario

reasons: Inferred cause is likely with low-cost minimal repair
No anchor conflicts; affected anchor remains compatible
Rollback plan covers reversible single-module change
Verifier + Release Gate required as next steps

notes: Worked example: a route_found candidate that repairs the dirty-footer invariant via an equality guard in the routing-role select. Three-step reversible route with a rollback plan; verifier + release gate required; no irreversible components, so no human authorization required.
